fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 15-year-old male referred by school counselor after journaling assignment revealed wishes to 'fall asleep and not wake up.'
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 15-year-old male who was sent to the ED after his English teacher noticed concerning content in a creative writing assignment where he wrote about wishing to 'fall asleep forever and escape the pressure.' Upon evaluation, patient acknowledges having passive thoughts over the past two weeks about not wanting to be alive, primarily when feeling overwhelmed by upcoming AP exams and college preparation activities. He describes his thoughts as fleeting wishes that everything would just stop, but he has no plan, no method in mind, and no desire to actually end his life. He recognizes these thoughts scare him and agreed when his teacher suggested evaluation. Sleep reduced to 5-6 hours nightly due to studying. He identifies strong support from his mother, enjoys playing violin in the school orchestra, and expresses clear goals about attending college. No previous mental health concerns or self-harm history. (Social Worker Marcus Johnson, LCSW)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Neat, wearing school uniform, appears tired
Mental_Status_Examination.Behavior: Polite, cooperative, somewhat anxious about missing class
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Passive suicidal ideation without plan or intent

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Adjustment disorder with anxious and depressed mood; passive suicidal ideation related to academic stress.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with mother and grandmother; high-achieving student enrolled in multiple AP courses; active in school orchestra; strong family relationships; clear future aspirations.
Assessment_and_Plan: Low risk: passive SI without plan or intent in context of academic overwhelm.
Multiple protective factors: family support, engagement in music, academic goals, good insight.
Safety plan developed focusing on stress management and reaching out when overwhelmed.
Referral to outpatient therapist specializing in adolescent anxiety and academic stress.
Mother educated on monitoring and reducing academic pressure.
Follow-up scheduled within one week; safe for discharge home.
fields.Author: Marcus Johnson, LCSW (Social Worker)
